patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket suppression is validated EXCEPT when pre-cap score=5 with all four grading dimensions (F-signal, SP-xERA, PQI, Stage agreement) simultaneously affirm -- 6/12 ARI@CIN was zeroed under this exact profile and won 5-2 unstaked, with a standing note that Claude should CONFIRM such picks to unlock the grade. Today's CLE@MIA matches this profile precisely and is CONFIRMed at the pre-cap PLATINUM level rather than treated as a generic SKIP.

HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD/DIAMOND) remains the single most-validated, non-negotiable rule in the archive -- overriding it upward has failed even on elite SP-edge stacks (6/4 Sale/ATL lesson: upgraded to GOLD anyway on an 'ace start' thesis, lost 7-2). It fires again today on ATL@STL (-11.61pp) and is not being re-litigated.

HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage) continues to be one of the most reliable caps in the stack, running 0-for-7 in the tracked backtest through 7/8 -- it fires today on KC@BAL and is treated as a firm DOWNGRADE, not a coin flip.

Thin-sample/developing-pitcher SP-xERA 'agreement' signals (the archive's term for pitchers like Cabrera, Weathers, Yesavage) have repeatedly misfired historically. Ryan Weathers (NYY@WSH) carries this exact profile today and earns no rescue credit despite a nominal SP-edge agreement.

Established aces show positive team-level track records independent of the model's own signals: Aaron Nola is 3-for-3 for PHI (5/9, 5/15, 5/26), Sandy Alcantara is 3-1 for MIA, Nolan McLean is 1-0 with a dominant start for NYM. All three recur today (PHI@DET, CLE@MIA, BOS@NYM) as qualitative caution flags against fading them.

7 of 15 games tonight are PENDING_SP_DATA in the diag CSV, but the live statsapi feed already confirms probable starters for all seven (Lopez, Quantrill, G.Rodriguez, Bieber, E.Rodriguez, Gordon, Greene/Imanaga) -- the pipeline just hasn't re-scored them yet (Statcast pitch-count gate and/or next run timing). Consistent with the known pre-dawn-slate fill-in pattern, not a data outage.

PHI @ DET.reasoning: DET (home) is a thin pre-cap score=1 pick: PQI runs -9.6 against it, and pick_prob=0.519 trips HARD CAP 10 (score 1->0). Archive precedent cuts against DET on two fronts -- Aaron Nola (PHI) is 3-for-3 as a positive signal for his team (5/9, 5/15, 5/26), and Flaherty's own SKIP-tier starts have also lost (6/12, DET fell 2-3 to CLE as a similar C-grade suppressed pick). CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: Nola 3-for-3 track record for PHI
PQI-against on bottom-bucket pick_prob

MIL @ PIT.reasoning: MIL (away) is a D-grade pick (score -2): Ashcraft's xERA runs 1.51 better than Sproat against the MIL pick, and PQI sits +15.9 against as well -- the textbook dual-opposing-signal pattern that has reliably predicted the opposing team's win across the June-July archive (e.g. 6/8, 6/14, 6/18). No batter-context data was populated for this game. CONFIRM the model's own SKIP.
lessons_applied: SP-xERA-against + PQI-against reliable loss predictor

NYY @ WSH.reasoning: NYY (away) reaches PLATINUM pre-cap but the stack is thin (score=2): only F-signal and Stage-agreement affirm, with no SP-xERA or PQI support, and pick_prob=0.533 trips HARD CAP 10 (score 2->0). Ryan Weathers (WSH) is explicitly flagged in the archive as a 'developing-pitcher SP-xERA misfire' risk (6/5 pattern note on Cabrera/Weathers/Yesavage), so there's no rescue case here unlike CLE@MIA below. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: Developing-pitcher SP-xERA signal unreliable (Weathers)
HARD CAP 10 needs 4-dimension affirm to rescue, not present here

KC @ BAL.reasoning: BAL's pre-cap B+ (score 3: F-signal, PQI confirms Δ+9.5, Stage agree) is undone by HARD CAP 7 -- BAL's own high-leverage bullpen is the weaker one (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.018 >= 0) on a tier-elevated pick, forcing score to 1 (B-, do not parlay). This cap has run 0-for-7 in the tracked backtest through 7/8, one of the most validated in the stack. DOWNGRADE the displayed GOLD to match the true post-cap grade.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 7 pick-side bullpen disadvantage validated 0-for-7

SEA @ TB.reasoning: TB (home) is a clean C-grade SKIP: PQI runs -3.6 against the pick, and Stage 1/2 disagree meaningfully (f5_full_delta=0.18), a resolution zone the archive has flip-flopped on all season with no stable rule. Luis Castillo (SEA) has one tracked instance where the calibrator badly overstated the edge (6/7, HARD CAP 6 hallucination at +49.79pp); today's +16.23pp is well under that threshold but still a caution flag on this specific starter. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: Stage 1/2 disagreement resolution unresolved (unstable rule)
Castillo matchup carries calibration-hallucination caution

CLE @ MIA.reasoning: CLE (away) is pre-cap score=5/grade A -- all four dimensions affirm (F-signal, SP xERA +0.56 agreeing, PQI confirms Δ-6.5, Stage agree). This is the exact configuration the 6/12 ARI@CIN postgame flagged: a pre-cap=5, four-dimension-affirming pick zeroed by HARD CAP 10 that then won 5-2 unstaked, with a standing note that Claude should CONFIRM such picks to unlock the grade. Sandy Alcantara (MIA) is 3-1 when tracked, a real caution, but doesn't outweigh the complete affirmation. CONFIRM at the pre-cap PLATINUM level.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 10 exception: pre-cap score=5 with all 4 dimensions affirming (6/12 ARI@CIN precedent)
Alcantara 3-1 track record as secondary caution

BOS @ NYM.reasoning: BOS (away) pre-cap score=1 is thin: PQI actually runs +5.8 AGAINST the pick, only F-signal and Stage-agreement affirm, and pick_prob=0.533 trips HARD CAP 10 (score 1->0) -- this does not match the CLE@MIA 4-dimension exception. Nolan McLean (NYM, home) has one dominant tracked start (6/17, +2.78 xERA edge, 9-1 win), arguing against fading him. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: PQI-against caps the stack below the 4-dimension exception
McLean has a dominant home track record

ATL @ STL.reasoning: ATL (away) stacks multiple caps: a real -11.61pp negative edge trips HARD CAP 1 (score 3->1) before HARD CAP 10 zeroes it further (1->0). HARD CAP 1 is the single most-validated rule in the archive -- overriding it has failed even on elite SP-edge stacks (6/4, Sale/ATL upgraded to GOLD on an 'ace start' thesis anyway, lost 7-2) -- and Kyle Leahy's one tracked start (6/12) was also a loss. CONFIRM the SKIP; no override warranted.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 1 non-negotiable even on elite SP-edge stacks (Sale precedent)
Leahy's one tracked start was a loss
